commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The PR should stay open for contributor proof or maintainer acceptance of the proof gap; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to apply.; Cleared: The diff changes docs plus a static policy conformance map/test and does not add dependencies, secrets handling, workflows, or code execution paths. (sha=3666a61d133755c39e115b9b86c81e77bd62bd3f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Skill Workshop and gateway tool-policy documentation, plus the policy extension's tool-group conformance map and focused coverage for agent tools.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +23, Docs +22. Total +56 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source drift, not via a runtime command: current main's core catalog includes the extra agents tools while the policy extension map on main still lists group:agents as agents_list plus update_plan. I did not run tests because this is a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR should stay open for contributor proof or maintainer acceptance of the proof gap; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to apply.

target.title: [codex] docs: clarify Skill Workshop tool visibility
target.body:
## Summary


- Sync the policy plugin's tool-group conformance map and add focused coverage so group:agents and group:openclaw match the documented goal-tool and skill_workshop expansion.
- Update the tools profile table so `coding` and `messaging` reflect the current core tool catalog, including `skill_workshop` and `bundle-mcp`.
- Add Skill Workshop tool-visibility guidance for agents where stricter profile, provider/agent policy, or `tools.deny` hides the manual `skill_workshop` tool.
- Sync the sandbox/tool-policy shorthand table so `group:agents` matches the same goal-tool and `skill_workshop` expansion.

Refs #87570.

## Verification


- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/tool-policy-conformance.test.ts` (blocked locally before assertions: incomplete Windows dependency install is missing `@rolldown/binding-win32-x64-msvc` after two `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` attempts)
- `git diff --check HEAD~1 HEAD`
- PowerShell exact policy group check for `group:agents` and `group:openclaw`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `npx oxfmt --check docs/gateway/config-tools.md docs/tools/skill-workshop.md docs/gateway/sandbox-vs-tool-policy-vs-elevated.md`
- `git diff --check`
- `rg -n "group:agents" docs/gateway/config-tools.md docs/gateway/sandbox-vs-tool-policy-vs-elevated.md`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: The public docs now describe the current `skill_workshop` and goal-tool visibility behavior consistently across the tool profile docs and the sandbox/tool-policy shorthand docs.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw docs checkout with the source docs and docs-list script.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `rg -n "group:agents" docs/gateway/config-tools.md docs/gateway/sandbox-vs-tool-policy-vs-elevated.md` after the docs edits.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the final docs grep:

```text
docs/gateway/sandbox-vs-tool-policy-vs-elevated.md:101:- `group:agents`: `agents_list`, `get_goal`, `create_goal`, `update_goal`, `update_plan`, `skill_workshop`
docs/gateway/config-tools.md:43:| `group:agents`     | `agents_list`, `get_goal`, `create_goal`, `update_goal`, `update_plan`, `skill_workshop`                                |
```

Observed result after fix: Both gateway docs now list the same `group:agents` expansion, including `get_goal`, `create_goal`, `update_goal`, `update_plan`, and `skill_workshop`.

What was not tested: I did not run a live Gateway or publish the Mintlify site locally. The local focused Vitest command was attempted but could not reach assertions because the Windows checkout dependency install is incomplete (`@rolldown/binding-win32-x64-msvc` missing after two `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` attempts).
target.author: sweetcornna

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: sweetcornna
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c5fe109329f5ee3fbd40eef2c91d2f788d3e40a3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a guarded Anthropic tool snapshot helper and a regression test so direct/custom tools with unreadable schema descriptors are skipped while valid sibling tools stay in the payload.

PR surface: Source +32, Tests +63. Total +95 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main reads direct/custom Anthropic tool schema fields without a guard, and a throwing getter on those fields would abort request construction; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The draft maintainer PR has a concrete correctness finding and overlaps an older open variant, so maintainers should choose the canonical patch shape before merge.
